methods.name: ClosePanel(Guid)
methods.signature: public static void ClosePanel(Guid panelId)
methods.summary: Will close or hide the specified panel type, in Windows Rhino, if it is the only visible tab the tab dock bar will be closed as well. In Mac Rhino it will always close the modeless dialog hosting the panel.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: Class type Id of the panel to close.

methods.name: FloatPanel(Guid, Panels.FloatPanelMode)
methods.signature: public static bool FloatPanel(Guid panelTypeId, Panels.FloatPanelMode mode)
methods.summary: Mac support: Display the specified panel in a floating window on Mac, the floating window will only contain the specified panel. Windows support: On Windows this will show or hide the floating container containing the specified panel. If the tab is docked with other tabs it will be floated in a new container.

params.name: panelTypeId
params.doc: Guid for the panel type to show/hide.

params.name: mode
params.doc: Show, hide or toggle the visibility state of the specified panel

returns.doc: Return true if the panel visibility state was changed, false otherwise.

methods.name: FloatPanel(Type, Panels.FloatPanelMode)
methods.signature: public static bool FloatPanel(Type panelType, Panels.FloatPanelMode mode)
methods.summary: Mac support: Display the specified panel in a floating window on Mac, the floating window will only contain the specified panel. Windows support: On Windows this will show or hide the floating container containing the specified panel. If the tab is docked with other tabs it will be floated in a new container.

params.name: panelType
params.doc: Panel type to show/hide.

params.name: mode
params.doc: Show, hide or toggle the visibility state of the specified panel

returns.doc: Return true if the panel visibility state was changed, false otherwise.

methods.name: GetOpenPanelIds
methods.signature: public static Guid[] GetOpenPanelIds()
methods.summary: Get a list of the currently open panel tabs in Windows Rhino, on Mac Rhino it will be a list of the currently visible modeless panel dialogs.

returns.doc: Returns an array of panel class Id's for the currently open panels, if there are no open panels it will be an empty array.

methods.name: GetPanel(Guid)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Obsolete method, use GetPanel<MyClass>(RhinoDoc)")] public static Object GetPanel(Guid panelId)
methods.summary: Will return an instance of a .Net panel if the panel has been displayed at least once. Panel instances are not created until a panel is displayed.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: Class Id of the panel to search for.

returns.doc: Returns the one and only instance of a panel if it has been properly registered and displayed at least once. If the panel has never been displayed then null will be returned even if the panel is properly registered.

params.doc: Runtime document Id associated with the requested panel.

methods.name: IsPanelVisible(Guid)
methods.signature: public static bool IsPanelVisible(Guid panelId)
methods.summary: Check to see if a panel is currently visible, on Windows this means you can see the tab, it does not necessarily mean it is the current tab.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: Class Id for the panel to check.

returns.doc: Returns true if the tab is visible otherwise it returns false.

methods.name: IsPanelVisible(Type)
methods.signature: public static bool IsPanelVisible(Type panelType)
methods.summary: Check to see if a panel is currently visible, on Windows this means you can see the tab, it does not necessarily mean it is the current tab.

params.name: panelType
params.doc: Type of panel to check for, this type must include a GUID attribute.

returns.doc: Returns true if panelType is non null and the tab is visible otherwise it returns false.

methods.name: IsPanelVisible(Guid, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool IsPanelVisible(Guid panelId, bool isSelectedTab)
methods.summary: Check to see if a panel is currently visible, if isSelectedTab is true then the tab must be the active tab as well.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: Class Id for the panel to check.

params.name: isSelectedTab
params.doc: This parameter is ignored on Mac. If Windows and true the panel must be visible in a container and if it is a tabbed container it must be the active tab to be true.

returns.doc: On Windows: The return value is dependent on the isSelectedTab value. If isSelectedTab is true then the panel must be included in a visible tabbed container and must also be the active tab to be true. If isSelectedTab is false then the panel only has to be included in a visible tabbed container to be true. On Mac: isSelected is ignored and true is returned if the panel appears in any inspector panel.

methods.name: IsPanelVisible(Type, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool IsPanelVisible(Type panelType, bool isSelectedTab)
methods.summary: Check to see if a panel is currently visible, if isSelectedTab is true then the tab must be the active tab as well.

params.name: panelType
params.doc: Type of panel to check for, this type must include a GUID attribute.

params.name: isSelectedTab
params.doc: This parameter is ignored on Mac. If Windows and true the panel must be visible in a container and if it is a tabbed container it must be the active tab to be true.

returns.doc: On Windows: The return value is dependent on the isSelectedTab value. If isSelectedTab is true then the panel must be included in a visible tabbed container and must also be the active tab to be true. If isSelectedTab is false then the panel only has to be included in a visible tabbed container to be true. On Mac: isSelected is ignored and true is returned if the panel appears in any inspector panel.

methods.name: OpenPanelAsSibling(Guid, Guid)
methods.signature: public static bool OpenPanelAsSibling(Guid panelId, Guid siblingPanelId)
methods.summary: In Mac Rhino this will currently just call OpenPanel, in Windows Rhino this will look for a dock bar which contains the sibling panel and add this panel to that dock bar as necessary, if the panel was in another dock bar it will be moved to this dock bar.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: The class Id of the panel type to open.

params.name: siblingPanelId
params.doc: The class Id of the sibling panel.

returns.doc: Returns true if the panel was successfully opened.

methods.name: OpenPanelAsSibling(Guid, Guid, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool OpenPanelAsSibling(Guid panelId, Guid siblingPanelId, bool makeSelectedPanel)
methods.summary: In Mac Rhino this will currently just call OpenPanel, in Windows Rhino this will look for a dock bar which contains the sibling panel and add this panel to that dock bar as necessary, if the panel was in another dock bar it will be moved to this dock bar.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: The class Id of the panel type to open.

params.name: siblingPanelId
params.doc: The class Id of the sibling panel.

params.name: makeSelectedPanel
params.doc: If true then the panel is set as the active tab after opening it otherwise; the panel is opened but not set as the active tab.

returns.doc: Returns true if the panel was successfully opened.

methods.name: PanelDockBar(Guid)
methods.signature: public static Guid PanelDockBar(Guid panelId)
methods.summary: Will always return Guid.Empty in Mac Rhino. In Windows Rhino it will look for the dock bar which contains the specified panel class Id and return the dock bar Id.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: Panel class Id for of the panel to look for.

returns.doc: Always returns Guid.Empty on Mac Rhino. On Windows Rhino it will return the Id for the dock bar which host the specified panel or Guid.Empty if the panel is not currently visible.

methods.name: PanelDockBar(Type)
methods.signature: public static Guid PanelDockBar(Type panelType)
methods.summary: Will always return Guid.Empty in Mac Rhino. In Windows Rhino it will look for the dock bar which contains the specified panel class Id and return the dock bar Id.

params.name: panelType
params.doc: Panel class for of the panel to look for.

returns.doc: Always returns Guid.Empty on Mac Rhino. On Windows Rhino it will return the Id for the dock bar which host the specified panel or Guid.Empty if the panel is not currently visible.

methods.name: PanelDockBars
methods.signature: public static Guid[] PanelDockBars(Guid panelId)
methods.summary: Will always return a empty array in Mac Rhino. In Windows Rhino it will look for any panel dock bars that contain the specified panel class Id and return the dock bar Id's.

params.name: panelId
params.doc: Panel class Id for of the panel to look for.

returns.doc: Always returns Guid.Empty on Mac Rhino. On Windows Rhino it will return the Id for the dock bar which host the specified panel or Guid.Empty if the panel is not currently visible.

methods.name: RegisterPanel(PlugIn, Type, String, Icon)
methods.signature: public static void RegisterPanel(PlugIn plugin, Type panelType, string caption, Icon icon)
methods.summary: You typically register your panel class in your plug-in's OnLoad function. This will register your custom call with Rhino, Rhino will create an instance of your class the first time your panel is created and embed this instance of your class in a panel container.

params.name: plugin
params.doc: Plug-in this panel is associated with

params.name: panelType
params.doc: Class type to construct when a panel is shown. If your class is derived from Eto.Forms.Control it will work on both the Mac and Windows version of Rhino. In addition Windows Rhino will support any class types that implement the IWin32Window interface or that are derived from System.Windows.FrameworkElement. Mac Rhino will also support classes that are derived from NsView. In addition to the type requirements the class must have a public constructor with no parameters or a constructor with a single uint that represents the document serial number and have a GuidAttribute applied with a unique Id. n Windows there is only one panel created which gets recycled for each new document. On the Mac a panel will be created for each open document and destroyed when the document closes. In certain situations in Mac Rhino a a panel may get created and destroyed multiple times when opening/closing a panel while editing a document.

params.name: caption
params.doc: Displays in the panel tab on Windows or at the top of the modeless window on Mac.

params.name: icon
params.doc: The panel icon. On Windows the panel may be displayed using the icon, caption or both. On Mac the icon will be used and the caption will be the tool-tip.

methods.name: RegisterPanel(PlugIn, Type, String, Icon, PanelType)
methods.signature: public static void RegisterPanel(PlugIn plugIn, Type type, string caption, Icon icon, PanelType panelType)
methods.summary: Call once to register a panel type which will get dynamically created and embedded in a Rhino docking/floating location.

params.name: plugIn
params.doc: Plug-in restringing the panel

params.name: type
params.doc: Type of the control object to be displayed in the panel

params.name: caption
params.doc: Panel caption also used as a tool-tip. On Windows the panel may be displayed using the icon, caption or both. On Mac the icon will be used and the caption will be the tool-tip.

params.name: icon
params.doc: The panel icon. On Windows the panel may be displayed using the icon, caption or both. On Mac the icon will be used and the caption will be the tool-tip.

params.name: panelType
params.doc: See PanelType

methods.name: RegisterPanel(PlugIn, Type, String, Assembly, String, PanelType)
methods.signature: public static void RegisterPanel(PlugIn plugIn, Type type, string caption, Assembly iconAssembly, string iconResourceId, PanelType panelType)
methods.summary: You typically register your panel class in your plug-in's OnLoad function. This will register your custom call with Rhino, Rhino will create an instance of your class the first time your panel is created and embed this instance of your class in a panel container.

params.name: plugIn
params.doc: Plug-in this panel is associated with

params.name: type
params.doc: Class type to construct when a panel is shown. If your class is derived from Eto.Forms.Control it will work on both the Mac and Windows version of Rhino. In addition Windows Rhino will support any class types that implement the IWin32Window interface or that are derived from System.Windows.FrameworkElement. Mac Rhino will also support classes that are derived from NsView. In addition to the type requirements the class must have a public constructor with no parameters or a constructor with a single uint that represents the document serial number and have a GuidAttribute applied with a unique Id. n Windows there is only one panel created which gets recycled for each new document. On the Mac a panel will be created for each open document and destroyed when the document closes. In certain situations in Mac Rhino a a panel may get created and destroyed multiple times when opening/closing a panel while editing a document.

params.name: caption
params.doc: Displays in the panel tab on Windows or at the top of the modeless window on Mac.

params.name: iconAssembly
params.doc: Assembly conataining the iconResourceId, if null it is assumed the iconResourceId is a starndard Rhino resource and the Rhino.UI assembly will be used. assembly will be used

params.name: iconResourceId
params.doc: The resource Id string used to load the panel icon from the iconAssembly. On Windows the panel may be displayed using the icon, caption or both. On Mac the icon will be used and the caption will be the tool-tip.

params.name: panelType
params.doc: See PanelType

properties.name: EtoPanelStyleName
properties.summary: Style applied to Eto controls hosted by the Rhino.UI.Panels and Rhino.UI.ObjectProperties systems.

properties.name: IconSize
properties.summary: Gets the panel icon size in logical units.

properties.name: IconSizeInPixels
properties.summary: Gets the panel icon size in pixels.

properties.name: ScaledIconSize
properties.summary: Gets the panel icon size in pixels with DPI scaling applied.

events.name: Closed
events.summary: This event is raised when a panel host is closed

events.name: Show
events.summary: This event is called when a panel is shown or hidden. This event will get raised multipThis times when the active document changes in Mac Rhino. It will called with show equal to false for the previous active document and with show equal to true for the current document. When the event is raised with show equal to false it only means the document instance of the panel is not visible it does not mean the panel host has been closed. If you need to know when the panel host closes then subscribe to the Closed event.
